exercises.name: Ben Bruno Teaches the Landmine Squat
exercises.description: Ben Bruno Teaches the Landmine Squat

exercises.name: Bench Alternating Leg Raise
exercises.description:
Lie flat on a bench with your legs extended off the edge. 

Raise one leg towards the ceiling while keeping the other leg hovering above the bench. 

Lower the raised leg slowly and alternate to the other leg, engaging your core throughout.

exercises.name: Bench Bridge March
exercises.alternateName: bench hip thrust hold with knee lift
exercises.description:
Start in a hip thrust position on a bench. 

Lift one foot off the ground, bringing the knee towards your chest, then lower it back down. 

Alternate legs in a marching motion, keeping your hips elevated and core engaged.

exercises.name: Bench Crunches
exercises.description:
Lie on a bench with your feet secured under the foot pads. 

Place your hands behind your head and curl your upper body towards your knees, engaging your abs. 

Lower back down with control to complete the rep.

exercises.name: Bench Hip Raise to Hip Lift
exercises.description:
Lie on a bench with your upper back and shoulders supported, arms extended behind your head holding onto the bench for stability. 

Keep your legs fully extended and together, hovering slightly above the bench. Engage your core and glutes to raise your hips off the bench (hip raise). 

From this position, lift your hips slightly higher toward the ceiling by engaging your lower abs (hip lift), keeping your legs straight and controlled. 

Lower your hips back down slowly to the starting position and repeat for reps.

exercises.name: Bench Hip Thrust
exercises.description:
Sit on the ground with your upper back against a bench, feet flat on the floor. 

Place a weight on your hips, then thrust your hips upward until your body forms a straight line from shoulders to knees. 

Lower back down, focusing on your glutes.

exercises.name: Bench Hopover Burpee
exercises.description:
Start standing on one side of a bench or elevated platform. Jump laterally over the bench, landing softly on the other side. 

Immediately drop into a burpee by placing your hands on the floor, kicking your feet back into a plank, performing a push-up, then jumping your feet forward and explosively jumping over the bench to the starting side. 

Repeat continuously.

exercises.name: Bench Jumping Step Ups
exercises.description:
Stand facing a bench and step one foot onto the bench. 

Explode upwards, driving the opposite knee up while switching feet in mid-air. 

Land with the opposite foot on the bench and repeat for a high-intensity cardio and lower body exercise.

exercises.name: Bench Knee Tuck to V Up
exercises.description:
Sit on the edge of a bench with your hands placed slightly behind your hips on the sides of the bench for support. 

Lean your torso back slightly and lift your feet off the bench with your knees bent. Begin by pulling your knees toward your chest (knee tuck), engaging your core. 

Then, extend your legs forward and simultaneously lift your torso to perform a V-up, bringing your upper and lower body together. 

Return to the starting position with control and repeat for reps.

exercises.name: Bench Leg Raise
exercises.description:
1. When performing this exercise, you'll want to ensure that your lower back keeps constant pressure into the bench.

2. You do so by keeping your abdominals engaged as your legs come back down towards the beginning position.

3. Lie with your back flat on a bench and your legs extended in off to the end of the bench. Keep yourself up by pulling on the bench. This will be your starting position.

4. As you keep your legs extended, straight as possible with your knees slightly bent but locked, raise your legs until they make a 90-degree angle with the floor. Exhale as you perform this portion of the movement and hold the contraction at the top for a second.

5. As you slowly lower your legs back down to the starting position, exhale to help keep the abdominals engaged.

exercises.name: Bench lower leg lifts
exercises.description: Try to keep back as flat as you can.

exercises.name: Bench Plank Single Arm Row
exercises.description:
Start in a plank position with your hands on a bench, shoulders stacked over your hands, and feet extended behind you. 

Hold a dumbbell in one hand. Engage your core and keep your hips as stable as possible while rowing the dumbbell toward your torso by bending your elbow, keeping it close to your body. 

Lower the dumbbell back down with control and repeat

exercises.name: Bench Plyo Push Ups
exercises.description:
Place your hands on a bench, slightly wider than shoulder-width, and extend your legs behind you so your body is in a plank position. 

Lower your chest toward the bench, then explosively push through your hands to lift your upper body off the bench slightly. 

Land softly back on the bench and immediately go into the next push-up. Keep your core tight and body stable throughout the movement.

exercises.name: Bench Push Up to Mountain Climber
exercises.description:
Place your hands on a bench, slightly wider than shoulder-width, and extend your legs behind you so your body forms a plank. 

Perform a push-up by lowering your chest toward the bench, then pressing back up to full arm extension. Immediately bring one knee toward your chest, then switch legs quickly to perform a mountain climber. 

Continue alternating legs for the desired number of reps while maintaining a tight core and stable upper body.

exercises.name: Bench Reverse Crunch
exercises.description:
Lie on a bench with your knees bent and feet off the edge. Use your core to lift your hips off the bench, curling your knees towards your chest. 

Slowly lower your hips back down, maintaining control to work your lower abs.

exercises.name: Bench Side Plank Hip Dip
exercises.description:
Get into a side plank position on a bench with your bottom forearm supporting your upper body and your bottom leg extended. 

Stack your top leg over the bottom for balance and place your top hand on your hip. Keep your core engaged and slowly lower your top hip toward the bench in a controlled motion (hip dip). 

Then, raise your hip back to the starting side plank position.

exercises.name: Bench Side Plank Oblique Crunch
exercises.description:
Get into a side plank position on a bench with your bottom forearm supporting your upper body and your bottom leg extended. 

Extend your top arm overhead and stack your top leg over the bottom for balance. Engage your core and, in a controlled motion, crunch by bringing your top hand toward your top knee, lifting your hip slightly toward the ceiling. 

Return to the starting position slowly and repeat.

exercises.name: Bench Side Plank Row to Press
exercises.description:
Get into a side plank position with your forearm or hand on a bench, keeping your body in a straight line from head to feet. 

Hold a dumbbell in your top hand with your arm fully extended toward the floor. Engage your core and pull the dumbbell up toward your ribcage (row), keeping your elbow close to your body. 

From the top of the row, rotate your arm and press the dumbbell upward until it’s directly above your shoulder. 

Lower the dumbbell back down in reverse order and repeat.

exercises.name: Bench Single Leg Hip Thrust
exercises.description:
Similar to the Bench Hip Thrust, but perform the movement with one leg extended out. 

This increases the challenge on the glutes and hamstrings of the working leg while testing your balance and stability.

exercises.name: Bench Skater Steps
exercises.description:
Stand facing the side of a bench with one foot on the bench and one foot on the ground. 

Push off the bench with the raised foot, switching feet in mid-air to land with the opposite foot on the bench, mimicking a skating motion.

exercises.name: Bench Toe Crunches
exercises.description:
Sit on the edge of a bench, leaning back slightly with your hands gripping the sides. 

Lift your legs off the ground and alternate tapping your toes on the bench, keeping your core engaged to maintain balance.

exercises.name: Bench Tricep Dip
exercises.description:
1) When performing dip on the bench, keep your elbows pointing straight behind you. 

2) Keep your body as close to the bench as possible as you dip down.

exercises.name: Bench Twist Crunches
exercises.description:
Perform a standard bench crunch but alternate bringing one shoulder towards the opposite knee. 

This targets the obliques as well as the upper abs, enhancing rotational strength.

exercises.name: Bench Up & Overs
exercises.description:
Stand beside a bench with one foot on the bench. 

Push through the foot to step over the bench, landing with the opposite foot on the bench as you bring your other foot down on the other side. 

Repeat in a continuous motion for a cardio and lower body workout.

exercises.name: Bench V Sit Leg Raise
exercises.description:
Sit on the edge of a bench with your hands placed slightly behind your hips on the sides of the bench for balance. 

Lean your torso back slightly and keep your legs fully extended and together, hovering just above the bench. Engage your core to lift your legs upward toward your chest while maintaining the V-sit position. 

Lower your legs back down slowly without letting your heels touch the bench, keeping tension in your core throughout the movement.

exercises.name: Bench V-sits
exercises.description:
Sit on the edge of a bench, balancing on your glutes. 

Extend your legs and lean back slightly, then pull your knees towards your chest while bringing your upper body forward. 

Extend back to the starting position, maintaining core tension.

exercises.name: Bent Knee Inverted Rows
exercises.description:
1. Start with your feet in front of the bar, heels on the floor and body in a straight line.

2. Grip the bar at shoulder-width, and pull your chest up towards the bar while keeping a stiff abdomen. 

3. Slowly return down towards the start position and repeat.

4. To make the exercise harder, lower the bar towards the floor.

exercises.name: Bent Knee Toes to Bar
exercises.description:
1. Start by hanging from a pull up bar with hands about shoulder-width apart. 

2. With feet together, slowly raise both of your knees up towards your upper body, pulling with straight arms.

3. When your knees are up to chest height, fully extend your legs so that your toes contact the bar in between your hands.

4. Slowly return to the start position before your next repetition.

exercises.name: Bent Knee Windshield Wiper
exercises.description:
1. When performing this mobility exercise, keep your knees bent at a 90-degree angle as you rotate your core from left to right.

2. Without forming an arch of your spine, lie down on an exercising mat.

3. Make a “T” shape of your upper body by extending your arms sideways. Raise your legs in a manner that your legs and knees form a 90 degree angle with the each other. This will be your starting position.

4. Raise your legs in an inclined/tilted manner such that both of your thighs are either touching each other or the lower one of them is touching the ground. Remember to exhale while you do this.

5. Pausing for a while, move slowly and controlled to the other side.

exercises.name: Bent Over Medicine Ball High Row
exercises.description:
Stand with your feet hip-width apart, holding a medicine ball with both hands. Hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back flat and core engaged, with the ball hanging just below your chest. 

Pull the ball explosively toward your upper chest or collarbone, driving your elbows outward and upward. 

Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the top of the movement, then lower the ball back to the starting position under control and repeat.

exercises.name: Bicycle Crunch
exercises.alternateName: Air Bike, core cross body crunch
exercises.description:
1) For the bicycle crunch, sit on your pelvis or your sit bones with your feet off the ground and upper body tall. 

2) Bring opposite elbow to the opposite knee. 

3) Twist between your pelvis and your rib cage.

exercises.name: Bird Dog
exercises.description:
1) When lifting your leg in this exercise, try to isolate the movement from your hip or your glute so squeeze your bum. 

2) Get into a 4-point position. 

3) Hands underneath your shoulders. Knees underneath hips. 

4) From here, make sure you squeeze your abdominals and make sure they are tight. Prevent any lower back arching. 

5) Lift the opposing arm and hand, slowly move as you lift.

exercises.name: Bird Dog Balance Row
exercises.description:
Start on all fours with a dumbbell in one hand. Extend the opposite arm and leg out while keeping your back flat. 

Row the dumbbell with your free hand, and then return to starting position. 

Alternate sides.

exercises.name: Body Weight Alternating Forward Lunge
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, step out far enough so you can drop out to 90 degrees throughout your lower body segments. Keeping your upper body over top of the back knee. 

2) Return to standing position and step out. 

3) Be sure that your front knee doesn’t lunge forward over top of your foot. 

4) Alternate and repeat.
